questions.question: Which of the following is not a factor that affects the entrepreneurship process?
questions.explanation: The book says three factors affect entrepreneurship: psychological, sociological, and situational. Cultural is not one of them.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Factors that Influence the Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: All the following are forces in the external environment of business except ______.
questions.explanation: Customers are not a macro-environment force. The book focuses on political, legal, technological, economic, and socio-cultural forces.
questions.reference: Anit 1, Session 4 - External Environment of Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Kwame Gyan, a successful entrepreneur believes that what he has achieved is as a result of his hard work, perseverance and dedication. Kwame is said to have ______ locus of control.
questions.explanation: Internal locus means you believe your own hard work and effort control your success, not luck.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Locus of Control

questions.question: The marginalisation of individuals or groups of individuals from the core of society is termed as ______.
questions.explanation: Negative displacement happens when people are pushed out of society's center and start businesses to survive.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Sociological Influences on Entrepreneurship

questions.question: The entrepreneur is the individual or team that leads and develops the venture. Respectively, which best describes the processes he goes through to build a venture?
questions.explanation: First find an opportunity, then gather resources, then build the organization. That is the correct order.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - The Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: Kwesi pursued a carpentry and joinery programme at Biriwa NVTI. Upon completion of his programme, he acquired some tools and a facility and started his own carpentry shop and now has two apprentices working with him. Kwesi can be classified as ______.
questions.explanation: Craft entrepreneurs use their hands-on skills like carpentry to start a business.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: In the locus-of-control theory, people who believe that's for the most part, the future is theirs to control through their own efforts are said to have ______.
questions.explanation: Internal locus means you believe you control your own future through your efforts.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Psychological Characteristics of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: Which phase of a new venture begins with initiation of sales activity and the delivery of product and services and ends when the business is firmly established and beyond short-term threats survival?
questions.explanation: The start-up phase begins when you make your first sales and ends when the business is stable and safe.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Phases of Venture Creation

questions.question: Under Ronstadt (1985) classification of growth, ventures that appear to have independence, autonomy and control as they are primary driving forces are classified as ______.
questions.explanation: Lifestyle ventures are about being your own boss and having control, not about getting huge profits.
questions.reference: nit 1, Session 4 - Growth Orientation of Ventures (Ronstadt)

questions.question: Buying into an existing firm and become part owner or shareholder with those that already exist can be termed as ______.
questions.explanation: Buy-in means you buy a share of an existing company and become a part owner with the current owners.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Options for Small Business Buyers

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the advantages of buying existing business?
questions.explanation: Ill will means customers or workers dislike the business. That is a disadvantage, not an advantage.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Buying a Business

questions.question: A business agreement in which a company grants other the right and license to sell a product or service and possibly to use the business system developed by the company is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Franchising is when a company lets someone else sell its products and use its way of doing business.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Meaning of Franchising

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the characteristics of a firm resources?
options: They can only be used once
questions.explanation: Resources can be used many times. Saying they can only be used once is wrong.
questions.reference: Unit 4, Session 1 - Nature and Characteristics of Business Resources

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the factors to be considered when assessing the credit worthiness of a firm?
questions.explanation: Factoring is a way to get money, not a way to check if a business is creditworthy.
questions.reference: Unit 6, Session 2 - Assessing Credit Worthiness

questions.question: According to Hirsch and Peters, which is the largest source for new business idea?
questions.explanation: Consumers are the biggest source of new business ideas. Listen to what customers want.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Sources of Business Ideas

questions.question: Which of the following is not a method of generating new business idea?
questions.explanation: Distribution channels are a source of ideas, not a method. Methods include brainstorming and focus groups.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Idea Generation Techniques

questions.question: Which of the following is not a psychological approach to entrepreneurship?
questions.explanation: Perception of desirability is about the situation, not psychology. The others are psychological traits.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Situational Characteristics

questions.question: All the following are approaches used to examine the entrepreneurial process, except ______.
questions.explanation: The book uses psychological, sociological, and situational approaches. Scientific approach is not used.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Approaches to Entrepreneurship

questions.question: Which section of a business plan establishes a favorable first impression of the owners and the business, and succeed in securing financing?
questions.explanation: The Executive Summary is the first thing people read. It must impress them to get your business funded.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 2 - Components of a Business Plan

questions.question: Which type of franchising can also be referred to as Business format?
questions.explanation: Pure franchising gives you the complete business system, so it is also called business format franchising.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Types of Franchising

questions.question: The components of the entrepreneurial process are ______.
questions.explanation: The four parts are: the entrepreneur, the opportunity, the resources, and the organization you build.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 The Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: The written outline of the entrepreneur's proposed venture, it's operational and financial details, its marketing opportunities and strategy, and its managers skill is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: A business plan is a written document that covers everything about your business: operations, money, marketing, and skills.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Meaning of a Business Plan

questions.question: A part of a business plan that is written last but is always placed at the beginning of the plan is the ______.
questions.explanation: The executive summary sums up the whole plan. You write it last but put it first so readers see it right away.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 2 - Structure of the Business Plan

questions.question: Kwame Ntow, a successful entrepreneur believes that what he has achieved as a result of his hard work, perseverance and dedication. Kwame is said to have ______ locus of control.
questions.explanation: Internal locus means you believe your hard work and dedication control your success, not luck.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Locus of Control

questions.question: The marginalization of individual or group from the core of society is termed as ______.
questions.explanation: Negative displacement is when people are pushed out from the center of society and become entrepreneurs.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 3 - Sociological Influences on Entrepreneurship

questions.question: An individual with no prior ownership experience but currently owns equity in a business is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Novice means beginner. A novice entrepreneur owns a business for the first time with no past experience.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Types of Entrepreneurs

questions.question: The following are roles of the entrepreneur in business except that they ______.
questions.explanation: Entrepreneurs DO accept uncertainty and risk. Saying they do not accept it is the wrong answer.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 1 - Role of Entrepreneurs in the Economy

questions.question: Which stage of a new venture starts at the initiation of sales activities and the delivery of products and services and ends when the business is firmly established?
questions.explanation: The start-up phase begins with first sales and ends when the business is stable and no longer in danger.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Phases of Venture Creation

questions.question: Who should write the business plan?
questions.explanation: The entrepreneur knows the business best, so they should write the plan. Others can help but not take over.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Responsibility for Preparing the Business Plan

questions.question: The purchase of a business, or a significant part of it, by its current management is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Buy-out happens when the current managers buy the business they already work for.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Options for Small Business Buyers

questions.question: Which of the following is not one of the importance of the business plan?
options: It helps determine the viability of the venture
It provides guidance to the entrepreneur
It serves as a reference to the competitors
questions.explanation: A business plan is for you, your team, and your investors, not for your competitors to read.
questions.reference: Unit 3, Session 1 - Importance of Business Planning

questions.question: Arrange the procedures for buying an existing business (Owens, 1990). I. Prepare a list of potential candidates II. Investigate and evaluate candidates III. Explore financing options IV. Ensure smooth transition V. Analyze your skills, abilities and interest
options: I, II, III, IV and V
V, I, II, III and IV
IV, I, II, III and V
III, I, II, IV and V
questions.explanation: First analyze yourself, then list candidates, investigate them, find money, and finally make the transition smooth.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 5 - Buying an Existing Business

questions.question: The following are advantages of starting your own small business, except ______.
questions.explanation: Hard, lonely work is a disadvantage, not an advantage. It is one of the bad parts of starting a business.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Small Business

questions.question: The following are disadvantages of starting your own small business, except ______.
questions.explanation: Needing less money is a good thing (advantage), not a bad thing (disadvantage).
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Advantages and Disadvantages of Small Business

questions.question: The critical factors of Pre-start-Up and start-Up Phases include: I. Relative uniqueness II. Relative investment size III. Availability of products
questions.explanation: All three factors matter in the early phases: how unique your idea is, how much money you need, and if products are available.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 3 - Critical Factors in Venture Creation

questions.question: The following are sources of new ideas except:
questions.explanation: Brainstorming is a method or technique, not a source. Sources are customers, existing businesses, and R&D.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 2 - Sources of Business Ideas

questions.question: The process of setting up your own business starts with ______.
questions.explanation: You cannot start a business until you first spot an opportunity. That is always the first step.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - The Entrepreneurial Process

questions.question: The characteristics of an individual that cause consistent patterns in behaviour overtime can be referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Personality is what makes you act in steady, predictable ways over time.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 2 - Entrepreneurial Personality

questions.question: According to Ronstadt (1985) classification of ventures, a venture that appear to have independence come and autonomy, and control as their primary driving forces and neither large scale more profits are deemed importance beyond providing a sufficient and comfortable living for the entrepreneur is termed as ______.
questions.explanation: Lifestyle ventures focus on being your own boss and living comfortably, not on getting huge profits or growth.
questions.reference: Unit 1, Session 4 - Growth Orientation of Ventures

questions.question: A business agreement in which a company grants others the right and license to sell a product or service and possibly to use the business system developed by the company is referred to as ______.
questions.explanation: Franchising is when a company lets someone else sell its products and use its way of doing business.
questions.reference: Unit 2, Session 6 - Meaning of Franchising
